title: Electric linear motor
abstract: The invention refers to an electric linear motor (14) comprising at least one linear stator (50) designed to be located in a fixed correlation to an environment, particularly building, and at least one mover (24, 26; 126) designed for connection with an element to be moved (16; 16a-16d) and co-acting with the stator, which motor comprises a stator beam (18; 114) supporting said at least one stator (50), which stator beam has at least one side face (42-48; 116-124) carrying ferromagnetic poles (52) of said stator spaced apart by a pitch (d), and which mover (24, 26; 126) comprises at least one counter-face (54) facing said side face(s) of the stator beam, in which counter-face electro-magnetic components (70, 71, 72, 74, 76) of the mover (24, 26; 126) are located.
claims: Claims Patent claims 1. Electric linear motor (14) comprising at least one linear stator (50) designed to be located in a fixed correlation to an environment, particularly building, and at least one mover (24, 26; 126) designed for connection with an element to be moved (16; 16a-16d) and co-acting to move along the stator, which motor comprises a stator beam (18; 114) supporting said at least one stator (50), which stator beam has at least one side face (42-48; 116-124) carrying ferromagnetic poles (52) of said stator spaced apart by a pitch (d), and which mover (24, 26; 126) comprises at least one counter-face (54) facing said side face(s) of the stator beam, in which counter-face electro-magnetic components (70, 71, 72, 74, 76) of the mover (24, 26; 126) are arranged to co-act with the ferromagnetic poles of the stator beam. 2. Electric linear motor (14) according to claim 1, wherein the stator (50) and the mover (24, 26; 126) form guide means for the travel of the element to be moved along the stator beam. 3. Electric linear motor (14) according to claim 2, wherein the ferromagnetic stator poles (52) of the stator beam (114) and the electro-magnetic components of the mover (70, 71, 72, 74, 76) form a magnetic bearing for the guide and suspension of the element (16; 16a-d). 4. Electric linear motor (14) according to one of the preceding claims, wherein the mover has separate magnetic bearing coils which are controlled independent of electro-magnetic mover components (70, 71, 72, 74, 76) of the linear motor, which are controlled to regulate the air gap of the linear motor, which separate magnetic bearing coils co-act with the linear stator, preferably with the stator iron. 5. Electric linear motor (14) according to one of the preceding claims, wherein the stator beam (18; 114) comprises a support structure (40) for at least two stators (50) and at least one fastening element (20) to fix the support structure (40) to the environment (12; 102, 104; 202, 204). 6. Electric linear motor (14) according to one of the preceding claims, wherein the elevator motor (14) is a flux-switching permanent magnet motor (FSPM). 7. Electric linear motor (14) according to one of the preceding claims, wherein the stator beam (18; 114) has at least two side faces (42-48; 116-124) with stator poles (52) having the same pitch (d) and wherein the position of the stator poles of both side faces in the length direction of the stator is preferably mutually offset. 8. Electric linear motor (14) according to one of the preceding claims, wherein the stator beam (18; 114) has a polygonal cross section and has several side faces (42- 48; 116-124) carrying ferromagnetic poles, which side faces are connected via corners. 9. Electric linear motor (14) according to claim 8, wherein the cross section of the stator beam (18) is rectangular, particularly square. 10. Electric linear motor (14) according to one of claims 7 to 9 wherein the stator beam (18) has four side faces (42-48) with stator poles having the same pitch (d) and the pitch of the opposite side faces is identical whereas the pitch of the side faces extending in right angles is offset in length direction of the stator, preferably by a half pitch. 11. Electric linear motor (14) according to one of the preceding claims, wherein the mover (24, 26; 126) has C-profile or U-Profile surrounding the stator beam (18; 114). 12. Electric linear motor (14) according to claim 11, wherein whereby the mover (24, 26) has four counter-faces (54) arranged in a rectangular configuration and facing the four side faces (42-48) of the stator beam (18), wherein preferably each of the counter-faces comprises the electro-magnetic components (70, 71, 72, 74, 76) of the mover and the opening in the C-profile is configured to accommodate (the) fastening element (20) of the stator beam. 13. Electric linear motor (14) according to one of the preceding claims, wherein the mover is configured have one mounting side for a rucksack-suspension of the element. 14. Electric linear motor (14) according to one of the preceding claims, wherein the mover (126) is in a direction perpendicular to the stator beam releasable from the stator beam (114). 15. Electric linear motor (14) according to one of the preceding claims, wherein a busbar (130) with conductor rails (132) is located along the length of the stator beam (114), and the mover of the element carried by it has at least one contactor (134), preferably with contact rollers, connecting the conductor rails. 16. Electric linear motor (14) according to one of the preceding claims, wherein the mover or the element (16; 16a-16d) to be moved has a power source (218), which is preferably configured as back-up power source for the mover (24, 26; 126). 17. Electric linear motor (14) according to one of the preceding claims, being configured to be installed in a high rise elevator with a vertical length of more than 50 m, preferably more than 100 m. 18. Electric linear motor (14) according to one of the preceding claims, wherein at least two parallel stator beams (18; 114) are located in the environment (12; 102, 104; each of which guiding at least one mover, whereby at least two movers (24, 26; 126) located parallel to each other are configured to be commonly connected to the element to be moved, each of the movers co-acting with one of the stator beams, respectively. 19. Electric linear motor (14) according to one of the preceding claims, wherein the ferromagnetic poles (52) are teeth provided on a side face (42-48; 116-124) of a ferromagnetic stator rod (50), which teeth (52) which are spaced apart by teeth gaps (53). 20. Electric linear motor (14) according to one of the preceding claims, wherein the stator(s) (50) does not have any permanent magnets and as well as no windings either.
